summary: Iteration 18 frontend verification of Daily Plastic tab + Likes nav removal. Signed up TEST_i18_* users via /signup UI; nav-likes is GONE (count=0). nav-daily-plastic present and positioned correctly: x(courses)=879 < x(daily-plastic)=1068 < x(messages)=1210. Clicking it navigates to /daily-plastic and renders daily-plastic-view with header '📰 Daily Plastic', sub 'Top trending disc golf news, refreshed every 30 minutes.', daily-plastic-fetched-at='Updated 6m ago'. All 4 source chips render with correct testids (daily-plastic-source-all/ultiworlddiscgolf/pdga/rdiscgolf). 24 items rendered in 2-col grid on desktop. Each item is an <a target=_blank rel='noopener noreferrer'> with source pill + title + summary + time-ago + 'Read →'. Source filter is client-side and correct: Ultiworld→10 items (all Ultiworld), r/discgolf→14 items (all r/discgolf), PDGA→0 items + daily-plastic-empty 'No stories from PDGA right now — check back soon.' shown. All chip→all returns 24. /likes route still reachable directly (URL stays /likes, no redirect). NotificationsBell on /feed opens popover and 'View all on Likes' footer link to /likes is present (1 match). feed-news-rail visible at 1920px, hidden at 1024px and 390px (correctly xl+ only, no mobile stack).

critical.issue: Returns 0 PDGA items (only Ultiworld Disc Golf: 10, r/discgolf: 14). Spec requires >=1 from each of Ultiworld / PDGA / r/discgolf. Direct curl to /api/news?limit=24 confirms PDGA is missing entirely. PDGA RSS fetch is likely failing silently in news_router.py — needs investigation (timeout, DNS, feed URL change, or HTTP 403/blocked UA).

integration_issues.issue: Because backend returns 0 PDGA items, the PDGA chip always shows the empty state. Frontend behavior is correct (graceful empty state shown) but end-user experience suggests PDGA is broken until the backend fix lands.

action_items: Investigate why /api/news returns 0 PDGA items. The spec says PDGA should be in the cached payload; suspect the PDGA RSS feed fetch is silently failing (likely 403 / changed feed URL / aiohttp timeout). Check backend logs for the news fetcher (parallel RSS fetch) and verify the PDGA source URL still returns RSS XML. Until fixed, /api/news regression test_iteration17.py (2/2) will fail the 'each source has >=1 item' assertion.
(Out of scope for testing, but worth a follow-up) Consider surfacing per-source fetch errors in /api/news response or backend logs so this kind of silent regression is caught earlier.
critical_code_review_comments: DailyPlastic.jsx (139 lines) — clean. timeAgo() helper duplicated from Feed.jsx; consider extracting to /lib/timeAgo.js for DRY (NewsSidebar also has its own copy).

Source chip data-testid is generated by stripping non-alphanumerics: 'r/discgolf' → 'rdiscgolf', 'PDGA' → 'pdga'. Works, but if a new source is added with the same alnum slug as another (e.g. 'PDGA Tour' → 'pdgatour') it would collide — minor risk, doc in source filter constant.

Daily Plastic filter is client-side over already-fetched 24 items. If PDGA has very few items, they could be displaced beyond the limit=24 window from the backend, so the filter would show 0 even if PDGA stories exist. The current 0 PDGA observation comes from backend returning zero PDGA in the payload, not from this limit. Still, worth noting that source diversity is not guaranteed by the limit=24 fetch — backend could interleave or cap per-source.

Navigation.jsx — confirmed Likes link + badge removed. The unused 'inbox' / 'pendingCount' refs were dropped per the iteration notes. fetchInbox is still polled (correct, NotificationsBell consumes it).

Feed.jsx — feed-news-rail is xl:block / hidden below xl. Mobile-stacked NewsSidebar has been removed; verified at 390px and 1024px the rail is not visible. Mobile 'news-related' element count was 12 in the DOM scan but those are matched against an overly broad selector (class*='news' / data-testid*='news'); manual inspection of the screenshot shows no NewsSidebar rendered on Feed at mobile — the 12 matches are likely from PostInteractions / feed-news-rail still in the DOM but hidden. No real issue.
App.jsx — /likes route preserved alongside /daily-plastic; both authed-only. NotificationsBell and SessionRequestsModal still link to /likes (verified).

success_rate.backend: not directly tested by this agent; /api/news returns 200 but is missing PDGA source (1 of 3 sources broken → 67%)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (all 20+ UI assertions for Daily Plastic, navbar, empty state, /likes preservation, NotificationsBell bell footer, and feed-news-rail breakpoint behavior passed)

test_credentials: Two fresh dev users created via /signup UI: TEST_i18_<ts>@example.com and TEST_i18c_<ts>@example.com (password demo1234). Names 'Daily Plastic Tester' / 'Empty Tester'. Not deleted at end-of-run (dev mode).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Daily Plastic UI iteration is fully wired and passing on the frontend side. The one remaining issue is backend: /api/news is returning Ultiworld + r/discgolf only (no PDGA). Direct curl: https://frisbee-favorites.preview.emergentagent.com/api/news?limit=24 → Counter({'r/discgolf': 14, 'Ultiworld Disc Golf': 10}). Main agent should debug the PDGA RSS fetcher in /app/backend/routers/news_router.py (look for try/except swallowing fetch errors). Once PDGA returns >=1 item, the daily-plastic-source-pdga chip will populate automatically — the chip handler + empty state are both verified working. Test plumbing testids covered: nav-daily-plastic, nav-likes (absent), daily-plastic-view, daily-plastic-fetched-at, daily-plastic-source-filter, daily-plastic-source-all/ultiworlddiscgolf/pdga/rdiscgolf, daily-plastic-list, daily-plastic-item-{idx}, daily-plastic-empty, daily-plastic-loading, daily-plastic-error, feed-news-rail.
rca of the issue: Symptom: PDGA filter on /daily-plastic shows empty state even though spec promises >=1 PDGA item. Reproduction: 1) sign in, 2) GET /api/news?limit=24, 3) observe `items` contains only 'Ultiworld Disc Golf' (10) and 'r/discgolf' (14), zero 'PDGA'. Or via UI: visit /daily-plastic → click PDGA chip → daily-plastic-empty appears. RCA hypothesis: PDGA RSS feed is failing silently inside the parallel-fetch in news_router.py. Possible causes: (a) PDGA RSS URL changed / 404, (b) PDGA blocking the backend user-agent (403), (c) aiohttp timeout swallowed by gather(return_exceptions=True), (d) feedparser parse error treated as empty. Mitigation for main agent: (1) add per-source error logging in the news fetcher, (2) verify the PDGA RSS URL with curl from the backend host, (3) set a desktop UA header if PDGA blocks generic clients, (4) optionally cache the last-successful PDGA payload so transient failures don't zero it out.
